description: Queue of identified corrections and additions to encyclopedia essays. Append new entries when section summaries or artifact extraction identifies essay errors. Set status to 'applied' after making the edit.

queue.description: Added -P/-F distinction: Ficino's anti-Averroist arguments in PT Book 15 derive from SCG 2.59.3-6 (Aquinas's paraphrase), not from the Long Commentary directly. Averroes's actual claim (-P) is that the intellect is not a body or power in a body; Ficino's charge (-F) is that Averroes denies the intellect is the substantial form of the body.
queue.evidence_source: Copenhaver, 'Ten Arguments in Search of a Philosopher,' Vivarium 47.4 (2009): 444-479

queue.description: Added paragraph to Kristeller section noting that Ficino's PT Book 15 Averroes arguments are mediated through SCG 2.59.3-6 and that Ficino attacks an Aquinas-constructed Averroes (-F), not Averroes's Long Commentary position (-P).
queue.evidence_source: Copenhaver, 'Ten Arguments in Search of a Philosopher,' Vivarium 47.4 (2009): 444-479

queue.description: Added note that SCG 2.59.3-6 is a specific transmission point: Ficino's hidden Thomistic dependence at the Averroes-argument in PT Book 15.
queue.evidence_source: Copenhaver, 'Ten Arguments in Search of a Philosopher,' Vivarium 47.4 (2009): 444-479

queue.description: Added paragraph distinguishing -P/-F positions after the Aquinas section. Also added Abulafia tzeruf (letter-combination) detail to the Oration ascent passage: the technique activates conjunction with the Active Intellect and is the functional mechanism behind the Kabbalah stage of the ascent.
queue.evidence_source: Copenhaver, 'Ten Arguments,' Vivarium 47.4 (2009) and 'The Secret of Pico's Oration,' Midwest Studies in Philosophy 26.1 (2002): 56-81

queue.description: The section on Averroes's Long Commentary position in paragraph 2 uses substantial-form language that conflates -P and -F. The description of Averroes's own argument ('But if the material intellect were the form or perfection of a particular body') still slips into -F framing. Consider revising paragraph 2 to be more precise about -P language.

Oration essay should incorporate Copenhaver 2002 Midwest Studies argument that the Oration is a program for Kabbalistic mystical self-annihilation (mors osculi as endpoint), not a dignity manifesto. Abulafia tzeruf as functional mechanism. Dignitas names angelic power, not inherent human value.
queue.evidence_source: Copenhaver, 'The Secret of Pico's Oration,' Midwest Studies in Philosophy 26.1 (2002): 56-81

queue.description: Dignity essay may overstate the humanist-dignity reading. Copenhaver 2002 argues dignitas names angelic power (capacity for self-transformation into any form), not inherent human value or uniqueness. The Oration's endpoint is mystical death, not dignity affirmation.
queue.evidence_source: Copenhaver, 'The Secret of Pico's Oration,' Midwest Studies in Philosophy 26.1 (2002): 56-81
